3.5 We recommended that management strictly enforce compliance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001 by requiring the submission of complete and
appropriate supporting documents such as detailed payrolls, computation
sheets, approved travel orders, itineraries, and at least three quotations from
travel agencies prior to the release of cash advances. Likewise, liquidation
reports be thoroughly reviewed and accepted only when fully supported by
required documents including plane tickets, boarding passes, and revised
travel itineraries to ensure the accuracy, validity, and proper authorization
of disbursements.

4.3 We recommended that management enforce the immediate liquidation of all
unliquidated cash advances and ensure that subsequent cash advances are
liquidated within the prescribed period of 30 calendar days for local travel,
60 calendar days for foreign travel, or immediately upon completion of the
purpose for which the cash advance was granted.

5.3 We recommended that management strengthen the monitoring and control
functions of the municipal accountant to ensure that all cash advances are
liquidated before additional advances are granted, in strict compliance with
COA Circular No. 2012-001.

6.12 We recommended that Management:
• Conduct physical count of inventory items by type shall be conducted
semestrally and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of
Inventories (RPCI).
• Prepare Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for every supplies issuances to the
end users and the submisssion of Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) with the proof of distribution to the Municipal Accountant
for the preparation of a JEV to record the expenditures using appropriate
expenditure accounts.
• Require the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments for
the distributed food supplies inventory.
Account Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustment ₱1,505,000.00
Food Supplies Inventory ₱1,505,000.00

7.4 We recommended that management instruct the Property Officer or the General
Services Office (GSO) to sign the acceptance portion of the Acceptance and
Inspection Report (AIR), in accordance with the provisions of the Manual on the
New Government Accounting System (NGAS), to ensure proper documentation
and accountability of government property.

8.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare or reconstruct the
Property, Plant, and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) using available
documents, such as, but not limited to, Purchase Orders or contracts for the
procurement of PPE, Disbursement Vouchers, and Acknowledgment
Receipts for Equipment, to facilitate the reclassification of PPE items costing
below ₱50,000.00 as expense or semi-expendable.

9.7 We recommended that Management:
• Direct the Engineering Office to evaluate the current status of the facility
and recommend immediate actions to repair or reinstate all damaged
electrical fixtures and lighting;
• Secure the facility by installing locks and assigning personnel to
safeguard the vicinity and the structures;
• Maintain cleanliness and prevent further deterioration; and
• Formulate and implement a utilization plan to ensure the facility serves
its intended purpose.

10.7 We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare detailed schedules of Accounts Payable and maintain copies of all
supporting documents such as Delivery Receipts, Acknowledgment
Receipts, Contractors’ Statements of Work Accomplished, Certificates of
Inspection and Acceptance, and other relevant documents that validate
recorded claims and are readily available for audit verification.

11.7 We recommended that Management promptly issue demand letters to the
concerned Implementing Agencies (IAs) and strengthen the enforcement of
fund liquidation by requiring the timely submission of duly approved Fund
Utilization Reports with complete supporting documents. Any unutilized
balances should be immediately refunded. Furthermore, Management
should ensure that no additional fund transfers are made to IAs with
outstanding unliquidated balances, in accordance with COA Circular Nos.
2016-002 and 2016-005.

12.6 We recommended that:
• The Municipal Treasurer require the Revenue Collection Clerks (RCCs) to
prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and remit all
collections in full to the Municipal Treasurer at the close of each business
day. For collectors assigned to field stations where travel to the
Treasurer’s Office exceeds one day, remittances should be made at least
once a week or immediately once collections reach ₱5,000.00, whichever
comes first.
• The Municipal Treasurer strictly verify all collections and accountable
forms turned over by the collectors. All collections, including those
personally received and those remitted by the RCCs, must be deposited
intact with the authorized depository bank on the same day or not later
than the next banking day, in compliance with Section 32 of COA Circular
No. 92-382.

13.4 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer prepare the
Report of Collections and Deposits in accordance with Book II, 2nd Edition, of the
Local Treasury Operations Manual.

14.6 We recommended the management to issue the Certificate of Final
Acceptance after One (1) year from project completion up to final acceptance
or the defects liability period or after repair of any defect if any to utilize the
securities in the advantage of the municipality.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make adjustments thru the JEV and provide the necessary details and reference for easy verification as required under Section 174 of GAAM Volume I .

We recommended that Management:
determine the market value and remaining estimated life of the buildings which will be the basis in providing the depreciation.
must be guided by PAS 16 and COA Resolution No 89-17 dated March 17, 1989  in assessing these properties.

We recommended that Management stop paying Government Share for HDMF contributions of Job Order Workers.
We also recommended that any amount of the Government Share which was not yet remitted to the HDMF be de-obligated and the Due to HDMF account be adjusted accordingly.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to record accurately the financial transactions of the Municipality.   Likewise, direct her to update cashbooks , determine the  balance, reconcile the same with the cash on hand, and certify  at the end of each month as required under Section 63 (D) of LTOM.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer record transactions in the cashbooks personally as required under Section 174 of GAAM Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile his records with the Municipal Budget Officer for fair and accurate reporting of the LDRRMF, including disclosures of details of the fund in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to viable and sustainable programs, projects and activities to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in  accordance with Department of Budget and Department of Interior and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No 2017-1 and Section 287 of RA 7160.

We recommended that management submit an acceptable justification why the old but servicieable gymansium was demolished. Henceforth, to  demolish properties that are  unserviceable and beyond economical repair only;  and request inspection by concerned  Auditor prior to demolition as required under COA Circular No. 89-296 to avoid loss or wastage  of government properties .

We recommended that MSWMB, in coordination with the Chairperson, require its members, the TWG and Secretariat to come up with strategies in order to be able to actively perform their duties and responsibilities in the implementation of MSWMP.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue demand letters to persons liable to enforce settlement of the suspensions and disallowances, otherwise, cause deduction of the said disallowances from the salaries or other receivables of the employees concerned.

We recommended that Management should:

a.	submit lacking documents, otherwise transactions will be suspended in audit; 

b.	ensure that all disbursements are duly supported with complete documentary requirements provided in Item 3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 before allowing payments; and

c.	require the barangays to submit liquidation reports as required under same circular.
observations.managementAction: Barangays who have implemented their fund transfers have submitted their liquidation reports and barangays who have on-going implementation have not yet submitted liquidation reports. The Delay in the implementation was due to the incapability of barangays to have public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management employ effective measures to expedite the implementation of the projects to provide beneficiaries of the early/optimum utilization of the program.

We recommended that Management should:

a.	Limit expenses for catering services/meals and snacks served during official meetings only up to the extent allowed under Section 343 of the Local Government Code of 1991, if the same cannot be avoided; and be reminded that the cost of meals and snacks spent for entertaining visitors/guests of offices concerned be charged to their monthly representation allowance which is the very purpose of such. 

b.	Ensure that meals, snacks, catering services for special occasions, conferences, and committee meetings are duly supported with invitations stating the agenda of the meeting, activity design, guest lists, copy of program, attendance and minutes, and that funds have been appropriated for the purpose.
We recommended that Management should strictly adhere with the 	requirements of the procurement law and give stern warning to all officials concerned against the commission of fraudulent acts.
We recommended that technical specifications on items to be procured 	should be provided to meet the needs of the procuring entity in clear and 	unambiguous terms.

We recommended that Management should deduct and withhold taxes 	due on the procurement of goods and services in accordance with Revenue 	Memorandum Circular No. 59-2009 and other applicable tax laws in 	order to avoid penalties that may be imposed for non-compliance.

We recommended that for future transactions, the Accountant should 	immediately compute and deduct taxes upon payment of financial 	assistance, salaries or any amount due to officials and employees subject 	to tax to ensure the continuity of cash inflows needed by the government 	in the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities.
